title: symphoricarpos rotundifolius var. parishii
intro: Symphoricarpos rotundifolius var. parishii, also known as the Snowberry, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Snowberry include the treatment of sore throats, respiratory issues, and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of Snowberry often include tea infusions and tinctures, which can be used to soothe coughs and promote wound healing.
